Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a DuckDB SQL query to filter an event log event_log table The filtering logic is exclude specific cases based on a pattern and a time constraint

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Table Name event_log Columns case_id activity timestamp and likely others but these are the key ones

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Filtering Criteria The Bad Cases to Exclude Condition A Sequence The case must contain the exact sequence of three activities in direct succession Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order Condition B Timing Within that specific sequence found in Condition A the duration between event 2 Create Order and event 3 Confirm Order must be more than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Determine the Logic Flow Step 1 Identify cases that satisfy Condition A the sequence Step 2 For those cases check if they satisfy Condition B the timing Step 3 Collect the set of case_ids that satisfy both A and B Step 4 Return all rows from event_log where case_id is NOT IN that set from Step 3

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Drafting the SQL Approach Selection Approach 1 Window Functions LAG LEAD This is usually the most efficient way for direct succession checks in SQL Approach 2 Self Joins Join the table to itself on case_id with offset indices or timestamp constraints This gets messy with ordering Approach 3 Pattern Matching DuckDB supports MATCH_RECOGNIZE This is elegant but might be overkill or less standard than Window Functions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets stick to standard Window Functions first as they are universally understood in analytics SQL Actually lets use Window Functions as they are robust

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining Approach 1 Window Functions CTE or Subquery 1 Ordered Data Select star with LAG activity over partition by case_id order by timestamp as prev_act LEAD activity over partition by case_id order by timestamp as next_act LEAD timestamp over partition by case_id order by timestamp as next_ts from event_log

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Filtering Logic The Exclude List We are looking at rows where activity is the middle element Create Order Check prev_act equals Approve Purchase Requisition next_act equals Confirm Order next_ts minus timestamp greater than interval 5 days If this returns any row for a given case_id that case_id goes into the exclusion list

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Constructing the Final Query with flagged_cases using SUM CASE WHEN activity equals Create Order and window conditions then 1 else 0 end as has_bad_pattern

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Constructing the Final Query with flagged_cases groups by case_id while also placing window functions inside the aggregate expression which is not a valid construction in standard SQL and DuckDB

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Optimization Clean up Wait the condition is Identify any case If a case has this sequence once the whole case is excluded So the logic is Find case_ids where exists a triplet matching criteria Select from main table where id not in step 1 Lets write the subquery for step 1 clearly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Select distinct case_id from a subquery that computes case_id activity prev_act next_act next_ts and curr_ts from event_log then filter where activity equals Create Order and prev_act equals Approve Purchase Requisition and next_act equals Confirm Order and next_ts minus curr_ts greater than interval 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Assembly Select star from event_log where case_id not in select case_id from ordered_events where activity equals Create Order and prev_activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition and next_activity equals Confirm Order and next_timestamp minus current_timestamp greater than interval 5 day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Edge Case Considerations What if there are duplicate timestamps The prompt implies a process log so strict ordering by timestamp is assumed Direct succession handled by LAG LEAD More than 5 days handled by interval arithmetic Exclude them handled by NOT IN or NOT EXISTS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Prompt Requirements Sequence Approve Create Confirm Direct Checked Time Create to Confirm more than 5 days Checked Action Exclude those cases return others Checked Output All columns of remaining events Checked

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Self Correction during drafting Should I use MATCH_RECOGNIZE It is available in DuckDB and is much more performant and cleaner for this specific sequence detection task

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: While MATCH_RECOGNIZE is cool Window Functions LAG and LEAD are more widely recognized by general SQL users and less prone to syntax errors if the user is on an older version of DuckDB I will provide the Window Function solution as the primary answer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Plan Use LAG and LEAD in a subquery to find the bad IDs then filter the main table Code structure Inner query adds prev_activity next_activity next_timestamp Middle query filters where sequence matches and duration matches and selects distinct case_id Outer query selects all from event_log where case_id not in that set
